headline: Carnival Drops 22% This Month As Unhedged Fuel Strategy Backfires
summary: CCL is up 3% as oil pulls back, but still down 22% this month. Carnival doesn't hedge fuel purchases, unlike RCL and NCLH. Oil spike may cut earnings by $0.20. Traders predict low chance of oil falling to $80 or normal shipping in Strait of Hormuz by April 30. Recession chances up to 34%. Carnival to report Q1 earnings on March 20, but unlikely to change fuel strategy. If oil drops, will Carnival lock in cheaper fuel or make same bet again?

headline: Cuba’s president confirms talks with U.S. — but warns an agreement will take time
summary: It comes at a time when the Caribbean island is grappling with a worsening economic crisis.

headline: Strait Of Hormuz: Buy The Fear Before This Waterway Clears
summary: Market fears of $150+ oil from Iran/Hormuz may be overblown. Learn why disruptions fade and which stocks could rebound (airlines, cruises, tech).
